summary: Reviewed 1 file(s) in pull_request target #1: The README update introduces poorly formatted command examples that may mislead users into executing incorrect commands due to concatenated text.

findings.title: Commands and descriptions are concatenated causing potential copy-paste errors
findings.description: The added lines combine shell commands and output/descriptions without separators, which could lead to incorrect command execution if users copy the entire line. For example, `$ mkdir ~/Hello-WorldCreates a directory...` would attempt to create a directory named 'Hello-WorldCreates...' instead of 'Hello-World'.
findings.evidence: Lines 2-4: '$ mkdir ~/Hello-WorldCreates a directory...', '$ cd ~/Hello-WorldChanges...', '$ git initSets up...'
findings.suggestion: Format commands and outputs as separate lines or use code blocks to clearly distinguish commands from text.
